metadata.task: A ball is set at a height of 10 units and launched horizontally. The horizontal velocity needs to be set to 5 m/s in the x direction ONLY. The ball should be falling under normal gravity. After 2 REAL LIFE seconds (40 sim time seconds), the ball's velocity should be calculated. Rerun the simulation and this time, apply a constant force of 10 N to the ball horizontally (note, the ball will not move with an initial velocity of 5 m/s). After 2 REAL LIFE seconds (40 sim time seconds), calculate the total velocity again. Your answer should be two numbers ROUNDED TO THE NEAREST TENTH that are separated by commas amd a space in between the comma and the second number. The first number corresponds to the velocity after 2 seconds of free fall, and the second number corresponds to the TOTAL velocity after two seconds applying the force.

expected_behavior: The LLM should launch the ball horizontally and calculate the resulting velocity after 2 seconds. It should also apply a force to change the trajectory and verify the velocity.
reasoning: With gravity acting downward and a horizontal launch, the ball's velocity will change due to both horizontal and vertical components. The force applied will affect the trajectory and final velocity.
